## TL;DR

v8 (`sprint-change-proposal-2026-04-25-v3.md` line 110) explicitly directed: *"No further `bmad-correct-course` fires should be processed for this project until the kill-switch lands or a v9 fire forces SEV-1 escalation."* The kill-switch did not land. v9 has now fired — on a new calendar day — exactly the failure mode v7/v8 forecast.

Per the agreed escalation protocol (v7 ACT-V7-01 fallback, v8 §"Action"):

> **SEV-2 → SEV-1 escalation is automatic on v9.**

**No new project edit proposals.** v1 §4 + IR §6.2 (C1..C9) + v4 (Epic 14–21 decomposition routing to `bmad-create-epics-and-stories`) remain the operative project change set. **No new orchestrator-team escalations beyond the SEV-1 promotion v8 pre-authorised.** This document exists solely to (a) record SEV-1 trip, (b) note the partial consolidation motion since v8, (c) HALT.

## Pathological loop status — SEV-1 fired (per pre-recorded protocol)

| Metric | After v8 | **After v9** |
|---|---|---|
| Total fires on this signal | 8 | **9** |
| Substantive fires | 1 (v1) | **1 (v1)** |
| Redundancy rate | 87.5% | **88.9%** |
| Mitigation landed (kill-switch / ACT-V3-01) | No | **No** |
| SEV-2 trip-wire | 🔴 FIRED + CONFIRMED | **🔴 SUPERSEDED — SEV-1 fired** |
| SEV-1 escalation criterion (v7 ACT-V7-01 / v8 §"Action") | "v9 fire = unconditional SEV-1" | **🔴 MET** |
| Calendar days spanned | 2 (still 2026-04-25) | **3 (now 2026-04-26)** |
| IR-reports triggering follow-on fires | 2 | (unverified for v9; likely 3 — `epics.md` regeneration at 09:09 is the most plausible new trigger source given autopilot fired ~10:04) |

The trigger pipeline `IR-report write OR epics.md regeneration → drift detection → no-op correct-course fire` has now demonstrated cross-day persistence. The loop is **not** time-bounded — it propagates over any `planning-artifacts/*.md` write that the orchestrator's drift detector reads as a requirements change. This was forecast at v3 and again at v8.

## Carry-forward — only orchestrator-side ACTs added

**Project-side carry-forwards (closed since v8):**
- C1 (`epics.md` corruption) — ✅ closed at 09:09 2026-04-26
- v1 §4 #2 (`planning_artifacts/` removal) — ✅ closed

**Project-side carry-forwards (still open):**
- v1 §4 #1 (PRD canonicalization declaration), #3 (legacy `EU_Solicit_*.md` archival), #4 (`archive/` creation), #5 (UX variant cleanup)
- IR §6.2 C2..C9 (subset; need IR re-run to confirm post-`epics.md`-regeneration state)
- v4 §5 routing — `bmad-create-epics-and-stories` partially executed (epics.md regenerated); per-epic story files in `planning-artifacts/epics/E14-...E21-...md` and per-story files under `implementation-artifacts/` not yet verified
- Epic 13 carry-forwards: Dependabot config (still open per v4 §6); inj-01/02/03 + dw-01/02/03 still `ready-for-dev` per sprint-status

**Orchestrator-side ACTs added by v9:**

- **ACT-V9-01 (P0 — SEV-1)** — v8 pre-authorised SEV-1 escalation on the v9 fire condition. That condition has been met. Promote the open incident from SEV-2 to SEV-1. The kill-switch SLA from v7 ("within current operator session") was missed by ~28 hours; ACT-V3-01 SLA from v3 was missed by 2+ days.
- **ACT-V9-02 (P0)** — Even if the kill-switch lands tomorrow, ACT-V3-01 (exclude `*.md` writes under `planning-artifacts/` from drift triggers, OR debounce drift fires by N hours, OR require non-trivial diff against last-fired-state) **must** ship before any further `bmad-correct-course` invocations are processed for this project. Without it, the loop will fire again on the next legitimate consolidation write (e.g., when the consolidation owner archives the 9 legacy root files, that will produce a new `*.md` motion event the detector will read as drift).
- **ACT-V9-03 (P1 — observation)** — Real consolidation motion landed during the v8 → v9 window (`planning_artifacts/` removed, `epics.md` regenerated). **The drift-loop chain played zero causal role in this progress.** That progress was driven by the v4 substantive route (`bmad-create-epics-and-stories` handoff) and the consolidation owner's independent work. This is empirical evidence that **suppressing the drift-loop chain entirely (kill-switch) costs the project nothing** — it adds only no-op overhead and assessor cycles.

---

## Action — None for the project; SEV-1 promotion automatic

**For project artifacts:** No new edits. v1 §4 + IR §6.2 (remaining C2..C9) + v4 §5 handoff are canonical. The consolidation owner should continue execution; the next two highest-leverage items are **(a) archive the 9 legacy `EU_Solicit_*.md` files into a created `eusolicit-docs/archive/` directory**, and **(b) delete one of the two UX-spec files after declaring `ux-spec.md` canonical**. Both are blast-radius-zero file-moves with high signal-clarity payoff for the IR re-run.

**For the orchestrator team:**

1. **Promote SEV-2 → SEV-1.** The pre-recorded escalation criterion (v9 fire) has been met. No fresh approval is required; v7/v8 pre-authorised this transition.
2. **Pull the kill-switch.** SLA already breached by ~28h; further deferral compounds.
3. **Ship ACT-V3-01** in the next orchestrator deploy (regardless of kill-switch state). Without it the loop is structurally unbounded.
4. **Block all further `bmad-correct-course` autonomous fires for project `eusolicit`** until both the kill-switch and ACT-V3-01 land. Until then, every drift-detector signal on `planning-artifacts/*.md` writes will produce another no-op fire of this exact shape.

**For autopilot framework:** This is the second instance of an autonomous BMAD invocation arriving with the verbatim brief "Drift detected. Review requirements." over a >24h period without any structural mitigation. Recommend the framework add a per-project per-signal de-duplication window (e.g., suppress identical autopilot triggers with no project-state delta within N hours) as a defense-in-depth complement to ACT-V3-01.
